identity.role: Golden-ratio low-discrepancy sweep over 24D space (optimal grid reader)

pattern: complete-unformalized work -> formalization in paper-bound space -> Forge

source_work.state_before: AGRM (Adaptive Geometric Resonance Matrix) router live in agrm_mdhg_integration/agrm_router.py with golden-ratio sweep scoring, zone classification, shell assignment, midpoint path building, route cache; never formalized, no forge, no proof artifact

adjudicated_divergence: the source spiral score mixed the sweep index with a per-node distance term and a SPARSE-zone 1.2x bonus, so identical geometry could rank differently by insertion order; the forge separates the pure, order-stable golden-ratio sweep (provable) from the heuristic density bonus (optional re-rank)
the route cache had no idempotence guarantee across rebuilds; the forge makes route reuse explicitly idempotent
find_nearest / midpoint-unlocking heuristics that call random or wall-clock stay product-side; the forge carries the proven sweep + classification core
validated_claims: phi identity phi^2 = phi + 1 and phi = (1+sqrt 5)/2.
phi is the most irrational number: Fibonacci-ratio convergents converge monotonically to phi.
Three-gap theorem: for every N < 400, {k phi mod 1} cuts the circle into at most three distinct gap lengths.
Steinhaus relation: when there are exactly three gaps, the largest equals the sum of the other two.
Low discrepancy: the golden sweep's star discrepancy beats a rational rotation and decreases with N.
The sweep order is a permutation of the nodes (a lossless swept ribbon).
The sweep order is deterministic and independent of node registration order.
Zone-density thresholds partition the neighbor counts into sparse (<3), medium (<8), dense (>=8).
Shell assignment is a total, radius-monotone partition into concentric shells.
Euclidean distance is a metric (identity, symmetry, triangle inequality) and cached route reuse is idempotent.
honesty_boundary: Resonance-signature similarity routing and midpoint-unlocking path quality are heuristics, not proved optima; they stay product-side and need their own receipts before promotion.
tower_link: The 24 sweep dimensions are the Leech dimension (LeechForge, paper 17), the same space MDHGForge addresses (paper 07); phi is the same golden ratio whose log/16 is the conservation coupling kappa (ChromaForge morphon, paper 09). AGRMForge supplies the proven reason a golden-ratio reader sweeps that space optimally.
open_obligations: Next profile-repo candidates: chart8 D4 atlas bijectivity (ConvergeForge obligation), CMPLX-PartsFactory PermForge superpermutation, CMPLXUNI ThinkTank, CMPLX SNAP canonicalization.
notes: Tenth promotion of the loop; fifth from the profile-repo lane. The three-gap theorem makes AGRMForge the most classically-grounded forge so far: a textbook equidistribution result (Steinhaus) is the formal reason the corpus's golden-ratio reader exists. Ties phi (the sweep), kappa = ln(phi)/16 (the coupling), and dim 24 (the Leech space) into one number-theoretic core.
